ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: The Inevitable Reality -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: The Inevitable Hour!

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: What is the Inevitable Reality?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: What is the Inevitable Hour?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And what can make you know what is the Inevitable Reality?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And what will make you realize what the Inevitable Hour is?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Thamud and 'Aad denied the Striking Calamity.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹Both˺ Thamûd and ’Ȃd denied the Striking Disaster.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So as for Thamud, they were destroyed by the overpowering [blast].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: As for Thamûd, they were destroyed by an overwhelming blast.

ayahs.textArabic: وَأَمَّا عَادٞ فَأُهۡلِكُواْ بِرِيحٖ صَرۡصَرٍ عَاتِيَةٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waamma AAadun faohlikoo bireehin sarsarin AAatiyatin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And as for 'Aad, they were destroyed by a screaming, violent wind
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And as for ’Ȃd, they were destroyed by a furious, bitter wind

ayahs.textArabic: سَخَّرَهَا عَلَيۡهِمۡ سَبۡعَ لَيَالٖ وَثَمَٰنِيَةَ أَيَّامٍ حُسُومٗاۖ فَتَرَى ٱلۡقَوۡمَ فِيهَا صَرۡعَىٰ كَأَنَّهُمۡ أَعۡجَازُ نَخۡلٍ خَاوِيَةٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Sakhkharaha AAalayhim sabAAa layalin wathamaniyata ayyamin husooman fatara alqawma feeha sarAAa kaannahum aAAjazu nakhlin khawiyatun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Which Allah imposed upon them for seven nights and eight days in succession, so you would see the people therein fallen as if they were hollow trunks of palm trees.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: which Allah unleashed on them non-stop for seven nights and eight days, so that you would have seen its people lying dead like trunks of uprooted palm trees.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then do you see of them any remains?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Do you see any of them left alive?

ayahs.textArabic: وَجَآءَ فِرۡعَوۡنُ وَمَن قَبۡلَهُۥ وَٱلۡمُؤۡتَفِكَٰتُ بِٱلۡخَاطِئَةِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wajaa firAAawnu waman qablahu waalmutafikatu bialkhatiati
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And there came Pharaoh and those before him and the overturned cities with sin.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Also, Pharaoh and those before him, and ˹the people of˺ the overturned cities ˹of Lot˺ indulged in sin,

ayahs.textArabic: فَعَصَوۡاْ رَسُولَ رَبِّهِمۡ فَأَخَذَهُمۡ أَخۡذَةٗ رَّابِيَةً
ayahs.textTransliteration: FaAAasaw rasoola rabbihim faakhathahum akhthatan rabiyatan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And they disobeyed the messenger of their Lord, so He seized them with a seizure exceeding [in severity].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: each disobeying their Lord’s messenger, so He seized them with a crushing grip.

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّا لَمَّا طَغَا ٱلۡمَآءُ حَمَلۡنَٰكُمۡ فِي ٱلۡجَارِيَةِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Inna lamma tagha almao hamalnakum fee aljariyati
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, when the water overflowed, We carried your ancestors in the sailing ship
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Indeed, when the floodwater had overflowed, We carried you in the floating Ark ˹with Noah˺,

ayahs.textArabic: لِنَجۡعَلَهَا لَكُمۡ تَذۡكِرَةٗ وَتَعِيَهَآ أُذُنٞ وَٰعِيَةٞ
ayahs.textTransliteration: LinajAAalaha lakum tathkiratan wataAAiyaha othunun waAAiyatun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: That We might make it for you a reminder and [that] a conscious ear would be conscious of it.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: so that We may make this a reminder to you, and that attentive ears may grasp it.

ayahs.textArabic: فَإِذَا نُفِخَ فِي ٱلصُّورِ نَفۡخَةٞ وَٰحِدَةٞ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faitha nufikha fee alssoori nafkhatun wahidatun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then when the Horn is blown with one blast
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: At last, when the Trumpet will be blown with one blast,

ayahs.textArabic: وَحُمِلَتِ ٱلۡأَرۡضُ وَٱلۡجِبَالُ فَدُكَّتَا دَكَّةٗ وَٰحِدَةٗ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wahumilati alardu waaljibalu fadukkata dakkatan wahidatan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And the earth and the mountains are lifted and leveled with one blow -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and the earth and mountains will be lifted up and crushed with one blow,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then on that Day, the Resurrection will occur,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: on that Day the Inevitable Event will have come to pass.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And the heaven will split [open], for that Day it is infirm.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: The sky will then be so torn that it will be frail,

ayahs.textArabic: وَٱلۡمَلَكُ عَلَىٰٓ أَرۡجَآئِهَاۚ وَيَحۡمِلُ عَرۡشَ رَبِّكَ فَوۡقَهُمۡ يَوۡمَئِذٖ ثَمَٰنِيَةٞ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waalmalaku AAala arjaiha wayahmilu AAarsha rabbika fawqahum yawmaithin thamaniyatun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And the angels are at its edges. And there will bear the Throne of your Lord above them, that Day, eight [of them].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: with the angels on its sides. On that Day eight ˹mighty angels˺ will bear the Throne of your Lord above them.

ayahs.textArabic: يَوۡمَئِذٖ تُعۡرَضُونَ لَا تَخۡفَىٰ مِنكُمۡ خَافِيَةٞ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Yawmaithin tuAAradoona la takhfa minkum khafiyatun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: That Day, you will be exhibited [for judgement]; not hidden among you is anything concealed.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: You will then be presented ˹before Him for judgment˺, and none of your secrets will stay hidden.

ayahs.textArabic: فَأَمَّا مَنۡ أُوتِيَ كِتَٰبَهُۥ بِيَمِينِهِۦ فَيَقُولُ هَآؤُمُ ٱقۡرَءُواْ كِتَٰبِيَهۡ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faamma man ootiya kitabahu biyameenihi fayaqoolu haomu iqraoo kitabiyah
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So as for he who is given his record in his right hand, he will say, "Here, read my record!
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: As for those given their records in their right hand, they will cry ˹happily˺, “Here ˹everyone˺! Read my record!

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, I was certain that I would be meeting my account."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: I surely knew I would face my reckoning.”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So he will be in a pleasant life -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They will be in a life of bliss,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: In an elevated garden,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: in an elevated Garden,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Its [fruit] to be picked hanging near.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: whose fruit will hang within reach.

ayahs.textArabic: كُلُواْ وَٱشۡرَبُواْ هَنِيٓـَٔۢا بِمَآ أَسۡلَفۡتُمۡ فِي ٱلۡأَيَّامِ ٱلۡخَالِيَةِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Kuloo waishraboo haneean bima aslaftum fee alayyami alkhaliyati
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [They will be told], "Eat and drink in satisfaction for what you put forth in the days past."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹They will be told,˺ “Eat and drink joyfully for what you did in the days gone by.”

ayahs.textArabic: وَأَمَّا مَنۡ أُوتِيَ كِتَٰبَهُۥ بِشِمَالِهِۦ فَيَقُولُ يَٰلَيۡتَنِي لَمۡ أُوتَ كِتَٰبِيَهۡ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waamma man ootiya kitabahu bishimalihi fayaqoolu ya laytanee lam oota kitabiyah
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But as for he who is given his record in his left hand, he will say, "Oh, I wish I had not been given my record
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And as for those given their record in their left hand, they will cry ˹bitterly˺, “I wish I had not been given my record,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And had not known what is my account.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: nor known anything of my reckoning!

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: I wish my death had been the decisive one.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: I wish death was the end!

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: My wealth has not availed me.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: My wealth has not benefited me!

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Gone from me is my authority."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: My authority has been stripped from me.”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Allah will say], "Seize him and shackle him.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹It will be said,˺ “Seize and shackle them,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then into Hellfire drive him.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: then burn them in Hell,

ayahs.textArabic: ثُمَّ فِي سِلۡسِلَةٖ ذَرۡعُهَا سَبۡعُونَ ذِرَاعٗا فَٱسۡلُكُوهُ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Thumma fee silsilatin tharAAuha sabAAoona thiraAAan faoslukoohu
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then into a chain whose length is seventy cubits insert him."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: then tie them up with chains seventy arms long.

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ لَا يُؤۡمِنُ بِٱللَّهِ ٱلۡعَظِيمِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Innahu kana la yuminu biAllahi alAAatheemi
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, he did not used to believe in Allah, the Most Great,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: For they never had faith in Allah, the Greatest,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Nor did he encourage the feeding of the poor.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: nor encouraged the feeding of the poor.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So there is not for him here this Day any devoted friend
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So this Day they will have no close friend here,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Nor any food except from the discharge of wounds;
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: nor any food except ˹oozing˺ pus,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: None will eat it except the sinners.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: which none will eat except the evildoers.”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So I swear by what you see
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Now, I do swear by whatever you see,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And what you do not see
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and whatever you cannot see!

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [That] indeed, the Qur'an is the word of a noble Messenger.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Indeed, this ˹Quran˺ is the recitation of a noble Messenger.

ayahs.textArabic: وَمَا هُوَ بِقَوۡلِ شَاعِرٖۚ قَلِيلٗا مَّا تُؤۡمِنُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wama huwa biqawli shaAAirin qaleelan ma tuminoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And it is not the word of a poet; little do you believe.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: It is not the prose of a poet ˹as you claim˺, ˹yet˺ you hardly have any faith.

ayahs.textArabic: وَلَا بِقَوۡلِ كَاهِنٖۚ قَلِيلٗا مَّا تَذَكَّرُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wala biqawli kahinin qaleelan ma tathakkaroona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Nor the word of a soothsayer; little do you remember.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Nor is it the mumbling of a fortune-teller, ˹yet˺ you are hardly mindful.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [It is] a revelation from the Lord of the worlds.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹It is˺ a revelation from the Lord of all worlds.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And if Muhammad had made up about Us some [false] sayings,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Had the Messenger made up something in Our Name,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: We would have seized him by the right hand;
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We would have certainly seized him by his right hand,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then We would have cut from him the aorta.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: then severed his aorta,

ayahs.textArabic: فَمَا مِنكُم مِّنۡ أَحَدٍ عَنۡهُ حَٰجِزِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fama minkum min ahadin AAanhu hajizeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And there is no one of you who could prevent [Us] from him.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and none of you could have shielded him ˹from Us˺!

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And indeed, the Qur'an is a reminder for the righteous.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Indeed, this ˹Quran˺ is a reminder to those mindful ˹of Allah˺.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And indeed, We know that among you are deniers.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And We certainly know that some of you will persist in denial,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And indeed, it will be [a cause of] regret upon the disbelievers.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and it will surely be a source of regret for the disbelievers.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And indeed, it is the truth of certainty.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And indeed, this ˹Quran˺ is the absolute truth.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So exalt the name of your Lord, the Most Great.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So glorify the Name of your Lord, the Greatest.
